description_long: This is a compact submachine gun with a utilitarian, industrial design, featuring a predominantly black metal body and a wooden pistol grip. The receiver and barrel are cylindrical, with a perforated barrel shroud for cooling and a simple, straight-line profile. The magazine is a prominent, vertical box type, inserted below the receiver, and the stock is a fixed, skeletonized metal structure with a slight curve for shoulder support. The iron sights are basic and integrated into the top of the receiver, with no optical attachments visible. The overall finish is matte with some wear, giving it a rugged, functional appearance suitable for urban environments.
description: A compact, black metal submachine gun with a wooden grip, box magazine, and skeletonized fixed stock. It has a utilitarian, matte finish and basic iron sights.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length, wooden grip, fixed skeletonized stock, and box magazine.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular barrel, muzzle, and the narrow profile of the magazine.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the first but with the wooden grip and stock more visible.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the end of the stock, the top of the receiver, and the alignment of the iron sights.
